steps.title: Rewrite in slope-intercept form
steps.content:
The answer choices are graphs, so rewrite the equation in slope-intercept form to see the line's intercept and direction. Start with $ax+by=b$ and solve for $y$:
$$by=-ax+b$$
$$y=-\frac{a}{b}x+1$$

steps.title: Interpret the slope and intercept
steps.content: Since $0\lt a\lt b$, the ratio $\frac{a}{b}$ is between $0$ and $1$. So the slope $-\frac{a}{b}$ is negative but greater than $-1$, and the $y$-intercept is $1$.

steps.title: Match the graph
steps.content: Look for the line that crosses the $y$-axis at $1$ and slopes gently downward from left to right. That is choice C, so C is correct.
